LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LARRY DUBAN'S MOTION FOR RESTRAINING ORDER IS DENIED FOR IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THIS ACTION IS THE BEST WAY TO RESOLVE THE MALPRACTICE CLAIM. THE COURT IS NOT PERSUADED THAT BERKO WAIVED HIS RIGHT TO A JURY TRIAL AND THE VAST MAJORITY OF DOLLARS IN THE INTERPLEADER RELATE TO THE PREVIOUSLY FILED DISPUTE BETWEEN DUBAN AND BERKO. OMAN'S CLAIM TO THE INTERPLEADER FUNDS SEEMS OF LITTLE CONSEQUENCE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CARLITO MANDIA, CSR 	689

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Restraining Order; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Dec-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LARRY DUBAN'S Motion For Restraining Order.
APPEARANCES: Bradley P. Kaplan, Esq. of O'Connor and Associates 693-9960 for deft & moving party Larry Duban; Daniel Berko, Esq. of Law Office of Daniel Berko 771-6174 deft & opposing party in propria persona (appearing via CourtCall).
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THIS ACTION IS THE BEST WAY TO RESOLVE THE MALPRACTICE CLAIM. THE COURT IS NOT PERSUADED THAT BERKO WAIVED HIS RIGHT TO A JURY TRIAL AND THE VAST MAJORITY OF DOLLARS IN THE INTERPLEADER RELATE TO THE PREVIOUSLY FILED DISPUTE BETWEEN DUBAN AND BERKO. OMAN'S CLAIM TO THE INTERPLEADER FUNDS SEEMS OF LITTLE CONSEQUENCE. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR 689
